stories.headline: Bitcoin spot volume crashes below $8B — lowest since October 2023
stories.tldr: Spot BTC volume slipped under $8 billion this week, the thinnest tape since late 2023, leaving prices exposed to violent moves.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Liquidity droughts historically precede sharp directional breaks — thin order books mean a single large flow can swing BTC several percent.
the_details: BTC traded $77,500 Wednesday after repeatedly rejecting the $80,000 ceiling earlier this week.
Total crypto market cap shed 0.6% to $2.64 trillion as the Fear & Greed Index sat at 26 (extreme fear).
BlackRock's IBIT led another session of net outflows from US spot Bitcoin ETFs.

stories.headline: CLARITY Act markup slips into May as Polymarket odds collapse 64% to 47%
stories.tldr: The Senate Banking Committee's market-structure markup slid past its April target into an undated May window, gutting passage odds.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Every week of delay pushes a US digital-asset framework deeper into election-year gridlock — the policy bid under altcoins is fading in real time.
the_details: Polymarket odds for a 2026 CLARITY bill passage fell from 64% in early April to 47% on Monday.
Over 100 firms including Coinbase and Ripple signed a letter pressing the Senate to schedule the markup.
XRP led the week's altcoin selloff, down ~4%, with BTC, ETH and SOL all 1–4% lower.

stories.headline: Lazarus Group tied to 95% of April's $620M crypto hack carnage
stories.tldr: On-chain forensics now attribute the Drift and Kelp DAO exploits — together $577M — to North Korea's Lazarus Group, the worst hack month since Feb 2025.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A single state-backed actor punching twin nine-figure holes in DeFi rewrites the threat model — bridge and admin-key risk just became geopolitical risk.
the_details: April losses hit $620M across 18 days, the worst single month since the February 2025 spike.
Drift Protocol was drained for $285M on April 1; Kelp DAO lost 116,500 rsETH (~$292M) on April 18.
Bridge failures and admin-key exploits dominated the attack surface, per Live Bitcoin News.

stories.headline: Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan debut as Consensus Miami sponsors May 5–7
stories.tldr: Two of Wall Street's biggest banks signed on as first-time Consensus sponsors, joining CFTC chair Selig and senator Ashley Moody on the May agenda.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Money-center banks paying to be in the room — not just speak — signals the institutional onboarding cycle has shifted from curiosity to commitment.
the_details: Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan are debut sponsors of Consensus Miami, May 5–7.
CFTC Chairman Michael Selig and Senator Ashley Moody headline the policy track.
White House crypto official Patrick Witt will appear at a Consensus event for the first time.

stories.headline: Polygon hard fork triggers Upbit POL deposit halt at 11:00 UTC
stories.tldr: South Korea's Upbit suspended POL deposits and withdrawals from 11:00 UTC Wednesday for a Polygon network upgrade focused on performance and scaling.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Korea is Polygon's deepest spot venue — even a clean fork can briefly break liquidity routes and amplify slippage on POL pairs.
the_details: Upbit halted POL deposits and withdrawals at 11:00 UTC on April 29, 2026.
The hard fork targets throughput improvements and prepares Polygon for further scaling work.
Trading remains live on Upbit; only on-chain transfers are paused during the upgrade window.
